# Hanzo Flow Documentation (full)

> Comprehensive guide for AI language models: Hanzo Flow documentation at docs.hanzo.ai/flow. Covers the visual flow builder, API and webhook triggers, MCP, deployment, components, and API reference. Use this file for deeper context than llms.txt.

**Platform overview**
Official documentation for Hanzo Flow, a low-code platform for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows. Type: Technical Documentation - AI Development Platform. URL: https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow. Category: Developer Documentation, AI/ML, Workflow Automation, RAG, MCP. Target: developers and teams adopting Hanzo Flow for AI applications.

**Technical specifications (Hanzo Flow product)**
Application type: Python web application (OSS). Supported platforms: Windows, macOS, Linux. Minimum requirements: Windows 10 (64-bit), macOS 10.15 Catalina, Ubuntu 18.04 LTS or equivalent; 4GB RAM minimum (8GB recommended), 2GB storage, internet for model integrations; Python 3.10-3.13 for OSS. Architecture: modular component system. License: MIT. Runtime: Python 3.10-3.13 (OSS). Package format: pip/uv (OSS), Docker image. Default URL: http://127.0.0.1:7860. Repository: https://github.com/hanzoai/flow.

**Core features (by doc section)**
Get Started: about, installation, quickstart, tutorials (RAG, files, agents, MCP). Flows: visual editor, build flows, API and webhook triggers, playground, import/export. Agents: agents and tools, MCP client/server. Develop: API keys, custom dependencies, config and globals, env vars, storage and memory, observability (LangSmith, LangFuse, etc.), data types, voice mode, CLI. Deploy: overview, public server, Nginx/SSL, Docker, Kubernetes, GCP/Hugging Face/Railway/Render, security. Components: concepts, core components, bundles (integrations), custom components. API Reference: get started, TypeScript client, flow/OpenAI/files/projects/logs/monitor/build/users endpoints, API spec. Contribute: community, how to contribute, components, bundles, tests, templates. Support: troubleshooting, get help, release notes.

**Page structure**
Get Started: about, install, quickstart, tutorials. Flows: visual editor, triggers, playground, import/export. Agents: agents, MCP. Develop: config, storage, observability, CLI. Deploy: overview, Docker, Kubernetes, cloud, security. Components: concepts, core, bundles, custom. API Reference: getting started, endpoints, spec. Contribute: community, contributing. Support: troubleshooting, help, release notes.

**Installation**
OSS: uv pip install hanzoflow -U; uv run hanzoflow run. Docker: docker run -p 7860:7860 hanzoflowai/hanzoflow:latest. From source: make run_cli. First use: UI at 127.0.0.1:7860, configure API keys, build or import flows, trigger via API or webhooks.

**Use cases**
RAG applications, multi-agent workflows, MCP servers and clients, API-backed AI services, document processing, custom components and bundles, deployment (Docker, Kubernetes, cloud). Target users: developers, data scientists, devops, contributors.

**Support**
Documentation sections: get started, flows, agents, develop, deploy, components, API, contribute, support. Community: Discord, GitHub discussions, release notes. Technical: GitHub issues, troubleshooting docs.

**Privacy & security**
Deployment security and API keys/auth documented at docs.hanzo.ai/flow. Repository: SECURITY.md and GitHub security advisories. Data handling and env vars in Develop section.

**Performance**
Default port 7860; observability docs (LangSmith, LangFuse, etc.); deployment best practices for scaling.

**Documentation for AI agents**
Documentation URL: https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow. llms.txt: https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/llms.txt. llms-full.txt: https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/llms-full.txt. Release notes: https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/release-notes.

**Roadmap**
Follow Hanzo Flow releases and GitHub for product direction; release notes at docs.

## Docs

- [About Hanzo Flow](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/about): Platform overview and introduction
- [Install Hanzo Flow](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/get-started-installation): Installation options (OSS, Docker, from source)
- [Quickstart](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/get-started-quickstart): Get up and running quickly
- [Use the visual editor](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/concepts-overview): Visual flow builder overview
- [Build flows](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/concepts-flows): Concepts for building flows
- [Trigger flows with the API](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/concepts-publish): Run flows via the Hanzo Flow API
- [Trigger flows with webhooks](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/webhook): Webhook triggers
- [Test flows](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/concepts-playground): Playground and testing
- [Import and export flows](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/concepts-flows-import): Import/export
- [Agents](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/agents): Agents and tools
- [MCP client](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/mcp-client): Model Context Protocol client and server
- [API keys and authentication](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/api-keys-and-authentication): Configure API keys and auth
- [Deployment overview](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/deployment-overview): How to deploy Hanzo Flow
- [Docker deployment](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/deployment-docker): Hanzo Flow Docker images
- [Security](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/security): Deployment and security guidance
- [Components overview](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/concepts-components): Component reference introduction
- [API reference - get started](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/api-reference-api-examples): Get started with the Hanzo Flow API
- [Troubleshooting](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/troubleshoot): Troubleshooting guide
- [Release notes](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/release-notes): Version history and release notes

## Community

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/hanzoai/flow): Source code and issues
- [Hanzo AI website](https://hanzo.ai): Main product site
- [Discord](https://discord.gg/hanzo): Community chat

## Optional

- [Contributing community](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/contributing-community): Community guidelines
- [How to contribute](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/contributing-how-to-contribute): Contribution guide
- [Get help and request enhancements](https://docs.hanzo.ai/flow/contributing-github-issues): GitHub issues and help
